observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Human Resource Management Officer – Designate and the In-charge of Office – Office of the Municipal Accountant strictly monitor and see to it that net take home-pay of employees shall not be less than the required minimum monthly net take home pay pursuant to the provisions of the General Appropriations Act for the year. Also, any deficiency in loan payments and other obligations as a result of the mandatory minimum requirement may be addressed through direct payment arrangements to be voluntarily made by the employees to the concerned institutions.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notices to the payees one month before the checks become stale. Stale checks shall be reported and attached to the RCI prepared at the period of cancellation;

For improperly reported stale checks, require the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and the Office of the Municipal Accountant to provide evidence that the checks are not outstanding that they have been voided and no obligation exist; and

Direct the In-charge of Office, Office of the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries for stale checks.

We recommended and the Management agreed to: 

Require the Municipal Officials and Employees to immediately complete the liquidation of the cash advances and ensure strict compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002; and

Require the In-charge of Office, Office of the Municipal Accountant to withhold the salaries of concerned personnel who failed to comply with the instructions on liquidation of their cash advances as provided under COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended and the Management agreed to secure the authority from the Sangguniang Bayan for the utilization of the lump sum appropriation without specific details appropriated under the 20% development fund. We further recommended that the MBO exercise prudence in the preparation of the executive budget and comply with the requirements by the Budget Operations Manual prior to submission to the Sanggunian for authorization through enactment of Appropriation Ordinance.
We recommended and the Management agreed to submit all the necessary supporting documents within the prescribed period and strictly adhere with Section 3 of COA Circular 2009-01.
We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure completeness of the required supporting documents prior to payment for all infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the Management require the OIC – Municipal Accountant to record the construction costs of infrastructure projects to the appropriate Construction in Progress account while under construction and to the appropriate asset accounts when completed.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive, Municipal Planning and Development Office and the Office of the Municipal Engineer strictly exercise proper planning, proper monitoring and timely implementation of the projects; and 

The Municipality strictly adhere with DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 in planning and implementing programs, projects and activities.
We recommended that the Management submit all the necessary supporting documents within the prescribed period and strictly adhere with Section 3 of COA Circular 2009-01.

We recommended that the Management require all the Department Heads to submit the POs with other supporting documents within the prescribed period and strictly adhere with Section 3 of COA Circular 2009-01.

We recommended that Management:

a.	Discontinue the practice of erroneously charging maintenance and other operating expenses of the LDRRMO to the LDRRMF; and



b.	Ensure that the disbursements charged against the LDRRMF are strictly in accordance with the NDRRMC DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
We recommended that the Management ensure that procurement of supplies and repairs and maintenance are not paid thru reimbursement and in strict compliance with the Revised IRR of the RA 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as head of the GAD Focal Point System, ensure the full implementation of the GAD programs, projects and activities of the Municipality as incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget.


We also recommended that the Technical Working Group (TWG) coordinate with the different offices of the LGU in the preparation, consolidation and submission of the GAD Plan and Budget.

1.8	We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Discontinue the practice of erroneously charging maintenance and other operating expenses of the LDRRMO to the LDRRMF; and
b.	Ensure that the disbursements charged against the LDRRMF are strictly in accordance with the NDRRMC DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

2.6	We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to the payees one month before the checks become stale. Stale checks shall be reported and attached to the RCI prepared at the period of cancellation;
For improperly reported stale checks, require the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and the Office of the Municipal Accountant to provide evidence that the checks are not outstanding that they have been voided and no obligation exist; and
3.	Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries for stale checks.

3.6	We recommended and the Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) obtain approval from the Sangguniang Bayan for the use of the lump-sum appropriation under the 20% Development Fund, which currently lacks specific project details.

4.9	We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:
The Local Chief Executive, Municipal Planning and Development Office and the Office of the Municipal Engineer strictly exercise proper planning, proper monitoring and timely implementation of the projects; and
b.	The Municipality strictly adhere with DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 in planning and implementing programs, projects and activities.

5.8	We recommended and the Management agreed to submit all the necessary supporting documents within the prescribed period and strictly adhere with Section 3 of COA Circular 2009-01 and Section 9 of COA Circular 2012 – 01.

6.6	We recommended and the Management agreed that the Human Resource Management Officer and the In-charge of Office – Office of the Municipal Accountant strictly monitor and see to it that monthly net take home pay of employees shall not be less than the required minimum monthly net take home pay pursuant to the provisions of the General Appropriations Act for the year. Also, we recommended and the Management agreed that any deficiency in loan payments and other obligations as a result of the mandatory minimum requirement may be addressed through direct payment arrangements to be voluntarily made by the employees to the concerned institutions.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the Inventory Committee conduct a complete physical count of all Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) of the Municipality and prepare the required report thereof.
 
Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile PPE items in the Reports of Physical Count of PPE with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments to present fairly the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following:

Management require the MGSO to maintain complete stock cards of the inventories and conduct periodic reconciliation with the ledger cards;

Form an inventory committee and conduct physical count every six (6) months, prepare a Report of Physical Count of Inventories and submit it to the Office of the Auditor; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting entries to restore back to the inventory account the undistributed/unconsumed items at the end of the year.

We recommended that the Management ensure the completeness of the required supporting documents before processing the cash advances and strictly adhere with Section 1.1.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended the following:

a.    The Municipal Mayor and the MSWDO establish well-defined guidelines on the grant of AICS and other financial assistance to indigent constituents and may adapt the DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 15 Series of 2022 thru a resolution; and  

b.     The Municipal Accountant ensure that documents attached to all claims under the AICS Program are complete including the case studies and the necessary certifications of indigency and in crisis situation.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive require concerned personnel or committee to conduct physical inventory of supplies and materials at least once a year and reflect the result in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI);

The Accounting Office correct their practice of treating inventories received as outright expenses by recording these procured goods to their appropriate inventory asset accounts; and

The Accounting Office and the Municipal General Service Office maintain Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively, to properly monitor and reconcile the inventories received and issued.

We recommended that the Municipality hire additional competent personnel to fill up the vacant position of LDRRMO following the composition and structure, position titles and salary grades set by Rule 6, Section 2 of IRR of RA No. 10121.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Inventory Committee conduct a complete physical count of all Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) of the Municipality and prepare the required report thereof;

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile PPE items in the Reports of Physical Count of PPE with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments to present fairly the PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive, Municipal Planning and Development Office and the Office of the Municipal Engineer strictly exercise proper planning, proper monitoring and timely implementation of the projects; and 

The Municipality strictly adhere with DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 in planning and implementing programs, projects and activities.

We recommended the following:

  The Local Chief Executive hire additional personnel to assist the Municipal Planning and Development Office and the Office of the Municipal Engineer in the proper monitoring and timely implementation of the projects; and

b. The Municipal Engineer prioritize the implementation of projects funded under the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as head of the GAD Focal Point System, ensure the full implementation of the GAD programs, projects and activities of the Municipality as incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget.

We also recommended that the Technical Working Group coordinate with the different offices of the LGU in the preparation, consolidation and submission of the GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive require concerned personnel or committee to conduct physical inventory of supplies and materials at least once a year and reflect the result in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI);



The Accounting Office correct their practice of treating inventories received as outright expenses by recording these procured goods to their appropriate inventory asset accounts; and


The Accounting Office and the Municipal General Service Office maintain Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively, to properly monitor and reconcile the inventories received and issued.

We recommended that the Municipality hire additional competent personnel to fill up the vacant position of LDRRMO following the composition and structure, position titles and salary grades set by Rule 6, Section 2 of IRR of RA No. 10121.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the OIC-Municipal Accountant take proper action to enforce the liquidation of outstanding cash advances.    For the deceased and for those who are no longer in service, after exhausting all possible remedies to demand liquidation of cash advances, they may apply for the writing off of the unliquidated balances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

observations.reason:
The LCE did not create an Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of supplies and materials.



Not Implemented

The Accounting Office still treat inventories as outright expenses in the accounting records.


Not Implemented

No reconciliation was done.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct a complete physical count of all Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) of the Municipality and prepare the required report thereof.
We also recommended that the In-Charge of the Municipal Accountant’s Office and the Property Custodian reconcile PPE items in the Report on the Physical Count of PPE with the accounting records and effect necessary adjustments to present fairly the PPE accounts in the financial statements.
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer prepare and attach the Statement of Time Elapsed and Percentage of Work Accomplished to every progress billing of infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the BAC Secretariat prepare POs using the prescribed form and ensure the required information are completely filled out therein.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the winning suppliers or their duly authorized representatives to clearly indicate in the purchase orders their date of receipt thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue the Certificate of Final Acceptance one year after the contract completion in order to observe the defects liability period within which the contractor assumes the responsibility to undertake the repair works, at his own expense, of any damage to the infrastructure on account of the use of materials of inferior quality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned personnel to:
Prepare the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) using the GPPB prescribed format, filling out each procurement project with complete information;
Submit the APP to the GPPB on or before the deadline of submission; and
Attach the authenticated APP to the disbursement vouchers for every payment of procurement activities of the Municipality.

We recommended the following:
a. The Local Chief Executive hire additional personnel to assist the Municipal Planning and Development Office and the Office of the Municipal Engineer in the proper monitoring and timely implementation of the projects; and
b. The Municipal Engineer prioritize the implementation of projects funded under the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as head of the GAD Focal Point System, ensure the full implementation of the GAD programs, projects and activities of the Municipality as incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget.
We also recommended that the Technical Working Group coordinate with the different offices of the LGU in the preparation, consolidation and submission of the GAD Plan and Budget.

Reiterated in CY 2021 AAR, Part II, Observation No. 1.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to include in the Bid Data Sheet/Request for Quotation a provision requiring the prospective bidders/suppliers to specify in their bid offers/price quotations the brand names of the goods they are going to offer and to include this requirement in the evaluation of bids or quotations.
observations.reason: The implementation of this recommendation is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly comply with the documentary requirements concerning payments of goods and services in adherence to COA Circular 2012-001 and other related issuances.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive, as the HoPE, use the Direct Retail Purchase method in its procurement of POL products, wherein delegation of authority to the end-user is permitted; and

The OIC – Municipal Accountant ensure that the Annual Procurement Plan and the resolution delegating such authority, if used, be attached as additional supporting documents to the disbursement vouchers relative thereto.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Local Finance Committee to regularly prepare the report on fund utilization and status of implementation of PPAs relative to the BGCM using the prescribed format, then, comply with the posting requirements.

We recommended that management:

Formulate the LGU’s APP in the GPPB prescribed format per project and program with complete information;

Submit the APP to the GPPB TSO every January including the supplemental APP; and

Attach the authenticated copy of APP to the disbursement vouchers for every payment of procurement activities of the Municipality.
observations.reason:
The APP contains incomplete information.

Not Implemented

The APP was not submitted within the prescribed period.

Partially Implemented

Authenticated copies of the APP were not attached to some disbursement vouchers of procured items.

Reiterated in CY 2021 AAR, Part II, Observation No. 5.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

The OIC – Municipal Accountant transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRM for CY 2016 – 2020 totaling ₱3.435 million recorded in the General Fund to a Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund Books for exclusive use for disaster risk reduction management activities within the next five years; and

The OIC – Municipal Accountant account for the unutilized/unexpended amount for CY 2014 – 2015 which remained in the General Fund in order for it to be treated as unappropriated surplus and made available for other social services after subsequent enactment by the Local Sanggunian.

We recommended that the MDRRMO designate prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF as certified correct by the OIC – Municipal Accountant, then, the same report shall be submitted to the Office of the Auditor by LDRRMO on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC), respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator carefully plan the programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% development fund and ensure that the target schedules of implementation as indicated in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) are met.
observations.reason:
Funds for development projects were not fully utilized.

Reiterated in CY 2021 AAR, Part II, Observation No. 6.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Chairperson, GFPS-TWG use the form of the GAD Accomplishment Report prescribed under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01, to properly evaluate the status of implementation of GAD programs, projects and activities.

We recommended that the Municipality hire additional competent personnel to fill up the vacant position of LDRRMO following the composition and structure, position titles and salary grades set by Rule 6, Section 2 of IRR of RA No. 10121.
observations.reason: The agency has not yet hired personnel to fill up the vacant position of the MDRRMO, nevertheless, the agency hired additional job order personnel to mitigate the lack of personnel.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive in coordination with the Municipal Planning Development Officer and Municipal Engineer properly plan the programs and projects to be undertaken by the municipality to ensure effective and efficient allocation of resources as well as timely implementation of project (Fully Implemented in CY 2020); and

The Local Chief Executive see to it that the 20% Development Fund shall be fully utilized for priority programs/projects/activities for the benefits of the constituents and the local government itself in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.
observations.reason:
Not all PPAs funded under the 20% development projects were utilized during the year.

Reiterated in CY 2021 AAR, Part II, Observation No. 6.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the OIC-Municipal Accountant take proper action to enforce the liquidation of outstanding cash advances.    For the deceased and for those who are no longer in service, after exhausting all possible remedies to demand liquidation of cash advances, they may apply for the writing off of the unliquidated balances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.reason: Demand letters were sent by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management immediately return to the Source Agency any unused balances upon completion of the project as required under Section 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to include in the Bid Data Sheet/Request for Quotation a provision requiring the prospective bidders/suppliers to specify in their bid offers/price quotations the brand names of the goods they are going to offer and to include this requirement in the evaluation of bids or quotations.

We recommended and Management agreed that they will strictly comply with the documentary requirements concerning payments of goods and services in adherence to COA Circular 2012-001 and other related issuances.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
The Local Chief Executive, as the HoPE, use the Direct Retail Purchase method in its procurement of POL products, wherein delegation of authority to the end-user is permitted; and
The OIC – Municipal Accountant ensure that the Annual Procurement Plan and the resolution delegating such authority, if used, be attached as additional supporting documents to the disbursement vouchers relative thereto.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the Local Finance Committee to regularly prepare the report on fund utilization and status of implementation of PPAs relative to the BGCM using the prescribed format, then, comply with the posting requirements.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
The Local Chief Executive require concerned personnel or committee to conduct physical inventory of supplies and materials at least once a year and reflect the result in the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI);
The Accounting Office correct their practice of treating inventories received as outright expenses by recording these procured goods to their appropriate inventory asset accounts; and
The Accounting Office and the Municipal General Service Office maintain Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively, to properly monitor and reconcile the inventories received and issued.

We recommended and Management agreed that they require the concerned officials to:
Formulate the LGU’s APP in the GPPB prescribed format per project and program with complete information;
Submit the APP to the GPPB TSO every January including the supplemental APP; and
Attach the authenticated APP to the disbursement vouchers for every payment of procurement activities of the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
The OIC – Municipal Accountant transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRM for CY 2016 – 2020 totaling ₱3.435 million recorded in the General Fund to a Special Trust Fund in the Trust Fund Books for exclusive use for disaster risk reduction management activities within the next five years; and
The OIC – Municipal Accountant account for the unutilized/unexpended amount for CY 2014 – 2015 which remained in the General Fund in order for it to be treated as unappropriated surplus and made available for other social services after subsequent enactment by the Local Sanggunian.

We recommended and Management agreed that the MDRRMO designate prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF as certified correct by the OIC – Municipal Accountant, then, the same report shall be submitted to the Office of the Auditor by LDRRMO on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC), respectively.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator carefully plan the programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% development fund and ensure that the target schedules of implementation as indicated in the Annual Investment Plan (AIP) are met.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Chairperson, GFPS-TWG use the form of the GAD Accomplishment Report prescribed under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01, to properly evaluate the status of implementation of GAD programs, projects and activities.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee, Municipal Accountant, and GSO/Municipal Treasurer to:

Prepare promptly the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and submit to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year;

Maintain the prescribed Stock Cards/Property Cards and SLC/PPELC, and continue reconciliation with the RPCPPE to establish the correct amount of the PPE in the financial statements; and

Observe proper disposal of unserviceable, obsolete, fully depreciated and no longer in use PPEs.

We recommended that the Municipality hire additional competent personnel to fill up the vacant position of LDRRMO following the composition and structure, position titles and salary grades set by Rule 6, Section 2 of IRR of RA No. 10121.

We recommended the following:

Material Recovery Facility should be provided or established by the Municipality in the New Eco-Center Facility to promote segregation of waste through recycling and composting;

The Municipality coordinates with the Department of Environment and Natural Resources and other government agencies in locating suitable landfill for the final garbage disposal of the Municipality; and 

The MENRO-Designate, in coordination with other offices of the Municipality remind the residents of the Municipality not to use open burning as the mode of garbage disposal.

We recommended the following:

The Local Chief Executive in coordination with the Municipal Planning Development Officer and Municipal Engineer properly plan the programs and projects to be undertaken by the municipality to ensure effective and efficient allocation of resources as well as timely implementation of project; and

The Local Chief Executive see to it that the 20% Development Fund shall be fully utilized for priority programs/projects/activities for the benefits of the constituents and the local government itself in accordance with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the OIC-Municipal Accountant take proper action to enforce the liquidation of outstanding cash advances.    For the deceased and for those who are no longer in service, after exhausting all possible remedies to demand liquidation of cash advances, they may apply for the writing off of the unliquidated balances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that management immediately return to the Source Agency any unused balances upon completion of the project as required under Section 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.
We therefore recommended that the Municipal Mayor purchase a cellular phone with a fixed plan commensurate to the needs of his Office.

We recommended that the Municipality hire additional competent personnel to fill up the vacant position of LDRRMO following the composition and structure, position titles and salary grades set by Rule 6, Section 2 of IRR of RA No. 10121.
observations.reason: Reiterated as Audit Observation No. 2, page 29 AAR 2019

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee, Municipal Accountant, and GSO/Municipal Treasurer to:
Prepare promptly the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and submit to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year;
Maintain the prescribed Stock Cards/Property Cards and SLC/PPELC, and continue reconciliation with the RPCPPE to establish the correct amount of the PPE in the financial statements; and
Observe proper disposal of unserviceable, obsolete, fully depreciated and no longer in use PPEs.

We recommended that the Municipality hire additional competent personnel to fill up the vacant position of LDRRMO following the composition and structure, position titles and salary grades set by Rule 6, Section 2 of IRR of RA No. 10121.

We recommended the following:
1.	Material Recovery Facility should be provided or established by the Municipality  in the New Eco-Center Facility to promote segregation of waste through recycling and composting.
2.	The Municipality coordinates with the Department of Environment and Natural Resources and other government agencies in locating suitable landfill for the final garbage disposal of the Municipality.
3.	The MENRO-Designate, in coordination with other offices of the Municipality remind the residents of the Municipality  not to use open burning as the mode of garbage disposal.

•	The Local Chief Executive in coordination  with the Municipal Planning  Development Officer and Municipal Engineer properly plan the programs and projects to be undertaken by the municipality to ensure effective and efficient allocation of resources as well as timely implementation of project.
•	The Local Chief Executive  see to it that the 20% Development Fund shall be fully utilized for priority programs/projects/activities for the benefits of the constituents and the local government itself in accordance  with the provisions of  DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee, Municipal Accountant, and GSO/Municipal Treasurer to:

•	Maintain the prescribed Stock Cards / Property Cards and SLC/PPELC, and continue reconciliation with the RPCPPE to establish the correct amount of the PPE in the financial statements; and

•	Observe proper disposal of unserviceable, obsolete, fully depreciated and no longer in use PPEs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LDRRM Officer and the OIC-Municipal Accountant perform the following in accordance with the prescribed provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012:

•	Religiously prepare and submit the required Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF by following the prescribe format described in Section 5.1.5 Annex B; and

•	Properly disclose on the Notes to Financial Statements using the prescribed format described in Section 5.1.16 Annex D to properly monitor the amount of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF to be transferred to the Special Trust Fund solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) within the next five years.
observations.reason: First raised as Finding No. 9 AAR 2015

observations.recommendation: We recommended that additional training courses for personnel and the involvement of community based DRRM shall be included in the Municipality’s LDRRM Plan as required under Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 2012 and Section 2 of Rule 10 of the Implementing Rules and Regulation (IRR) of Republic Act 10121.

We recommended that the Municipality completely develop and maintain a Gender and Development (GAD) database for gender statistics and sex-disaggregation data as required under Republic Act (RA) 9710 and its Implementing Rules  and Regulations (IRR), Memorandum Circular No. 2014-05 of the Philippine Commission on Women (PCW) and DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular  No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Municipality should strictly comply with the provision of Section 5 (2) of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 and follow the procedural guidelines in the submission, review and approval of LGUs’ GAD plans and budget.
We therefore recommend that the Municipal Mayor purchase a cellular phone with a fixed plan commensurate to the needs of his Office.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has lot of transactions that require to call not just calling one network subscriber thus getting a fixed plan would increase the expenses since plan are only offered for same networks and not to both networks.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee, Municipal Accountant, and GSO/Municipal Treasurer to:
Maintain the prescribed Stock Cards/Property Cards and SLC/PPELC, and continue reconciliation with the RPCPPE to establish the correct amount of the PPE in the financial statements; and
Observe proper disposal of unserviceable, obsolete, fully depreciated and no longer in use PPEs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the OIC-Municipal Accountant take proper action to enforce the liquidation of outstanding cash advances.    For the deceased and for those who are no longer in service, after exhausting all possible remedies to demand liquidation of cash advances, they may apply for the writing off of the unliquidated balances in accordance to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that management immediately return to the Source Agency any unused balances upon completion of the project as required under Section 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer and the OIC-Municipal Accountant perform the following in accordance with the prescribed provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012:
Religiously prepare and submit the required Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF by following the prescribe format described in Section 5.1.5 Annex B; and
Properly disclose on the Notes to Financial Statements using the prescribed format described in Section 5.1.16 Annex D to properly monitor the amount of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF to be transferred to the Special Trust Fund solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) within the next five years.

We recommended that the Municipality hire additional competent personnel to fill up the vacant position of LDRRMO following the composition and structure, position titles and salary grades set by Rule 6, Section 2 of IRR of RA No. 10121.

We recommended that additional training courses for personnel and the involvement of community based DRRM shall be included in the Municipality’s LDRRM Plan as required under Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 2012 and Section 2 of Rule 10 of the Implementing Rules and Regulation (IRR) of Republic Act 10121.

We recommended that the Municipality completely develop and maintain a Gender and Development (GAD) database for gender statistics and sex-disaggregation data as required under Republic Act (RA) 9710 and its Implementing Rules  and Regulations (IRR), Memorandum Circular No. 2014-05 of the Philippine Commission on Women (PCW) and DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular  No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee, Municipal Accountant, and GSO/Municipal Treasurer to:

•	Prepare promptly the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and submit to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year;

•	Maintain the prescribed stock cards/property cards and SLC/PPELC, and reconcile it with the RPCPPE to establish the correct amount of the PPE in the financial statements; and

•	Observe proper disposal of unserviceable, obsolete, fully depreciated and no longer in use PPEs.
observations.managementAction: Agency conducted inventory but reconciliation is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor take proper action to enforce the liquidation of outstanding cash advances.  Require personnel who have copies of their liquidation documents to submit the documents to the Officer-in-Charge of the Municipal Accountant’s Office to liquidate their cash advances.  For the deceased and for those who are no longer in service, after exhausting all possible remedies to demand liquidation of cash advances, the agency may apply for the writing off of the unliquidated balances in accordance to COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Accountant be directed to comply with the provisions of Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 100 and 122 of PD 1445 and Section 70 of Manual on NGAS for Local Government Units and pertinent COA Circulars in the submission of accounts and financial reports for the conduct of timely audit.

We recommended that management should strictly comply with the reporting requirements on the fund utilization pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.  In particular, the Municipal Accountant should timely prepare the required monthly report and the same shall be submitted to the office of the Auditor by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (LDRRMO) on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC).
observations.managementAction: Management submits report but with a different format.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board through the MENRO follow up the status of the approval of the plan as required under Section 16 of RA 9003.

We recommended that management refrain from charging to the Special Education Fund (SEF) expenditures that do not fall within the specific allowable expenditures as provided in Section 4.1 of DEPED, DBM, and DILG Joint Circular  (JC) No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that the Municipality should strictly comply with the provision of Section 5 (2) of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 and follow the procedural guidelines in the submission, review and approval of LGUs’ GAD plans and budget.

We recommended the following:

•	The management indicate the complete details/technical specifications in every Purchase Requests (PRs) and Purchase Orders (POs) in order to make sure that the goods procured are of good quality and meet the needs/requirements of the end-users; and

•	The management indicates the cost estimate in the PRs and includes the amount of the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) in the Request for Quotation (RFQ) sent out to suppliers/contractors.  This is to provide for the ceiling of the prices of the goods that would enable the Municipality to attain the most advantageous price prevailing in the market and to promote fair competition among the prospective suppliers/contractors.

•	The management ensures that complete documentary requirements are attached to the DVs in succeeding transactions to substantiate the validity of the transactions and to facilitate post-audit.
We therefore recommend that the Municipal Mayor purchase a cellular phone with a fixed plan commensurate to the needs of his Office.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has lot of transactions that require to call not just calling one network subscriber thus getting a fixed plan would increase the expenses since plan are only offered for same networks and not to both networks.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee, Municipal Accountant, and GSO/Municipal Treasurer to:
Prepare promptly the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and submit to the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year;
Maintain the prescribed stock cards/property cards and SLC/PPELC, and reconcile it with the RPCPPE to establish the correct amount of the PPE in the financial statements; and
Observe proper disposal of unserviceable, obsolete, fully depreciated and no longer in use PPEs.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor take proper action to enforce the liquidation of outstanding cash advances.  Require personnel who have copies of their liquidation documents to submit the documents to the Officer-in-Charge of the Municipal Accountant’s Office to liquidate their cash advances.  For the deceased and for those who are no longer in service, after exhausting all possible remedies to demand liquidation of cash advances, the agency may apply for the writing off of the unliquidated balances in accordance to COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Accountant be directed to comply with the provisions of Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 100 and 122 of PD 1445 and Section 70 of Manual on NGAS for Local Government Units and pertinent COA Circulars in the submission of accounts and financial reports for the conduct of timely audit.

We recommended that management should strictly comply with the reporting requirements on the fund utilization pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.  In particular, the Municipal Accountant should timely prepare the required monthly report and the same shall be submitted to the office of the Auditor by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (LDRRMO) on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC).
We recommended that the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board through the MENRO follow up the status of the approval of the plan as required under Section 16 of RA 9003.

We recommended that management refrain from charging to the Special Education Fund (SEF) expenditures that do not fall within the specific allowable expenditures as provided in Section 4.1 of DEPED, DBM, and DILG Joint Circular  (JC) No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that the Municipality should strictly comply with the provision of Section 5 (2) of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 and follow the procedural guidelines in the submission, review and approval of LGUs’ GAD plans and budget.

We recommended that accounting and property records be reconciled in accordance with COA Circular No. 91-368 and Section 114 of the New Government Accounting System Manual for LGUs for a more reliable balance of PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Agency conducted inventory but failed to submit the RPCPPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management make sure that complete documentary requirements are attached to the Disbursement Vouchers (DVs) in succeeding transactions to substantiate the validity of the transactions and to facilitate post-audit.  Likewise, ensure that procurement opportunities, results and other related information are duly communicated to interested parties by posting in the PhilGEPS electronic bulletin board and submitting proof thereof for post-audit purposes.

We recommended the following:

•	The management indicate the complete details/technical specifications in every Purchase Requests (PRs) and Purchase Orders (POs) in order to make sure that the goods procured are of good quality and meet the needs/requirements of the end-users; and

•	The management indicate the cost estimate in the PRs and include the amount of the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) in the Request for Quotation (RFQ) sent out to suppliers/contractors.  This is to provide for the ceiling of the prices of the goods that would enable the Municipality to attain the most advantageous price prevailing in the market and to promote fair competition among the prospective suppliers/contractors.

•	The management ensure that complete documentary requirements are attached to the DVs in succeeding transactions to substantiate the validity of the transactions and to facilitate post-audit.

We recommended the following:

•	The management follow the provisions of RA No. 9184 and refrain from making references to brand names during procurement;
•	The management comply with the provisions of the National Telecommunications Commission (NTC) Memorandum Circulars (MCs) and secure the necessary permits needed to purchase and possess radio communication equipment.  Likewise, ensure that future purchases of such equipment are only made to suppliers with appropriate permits; and
•	The management submit the lacking documentary requirements and ensure that complete documentary requirements are attached to the DVs in succeeding transactions to facilitate post-audit.  Ensure that the transactions are valid and properly approved.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate properly check the transactions as to completeness of supporting documents.  In the case of the above listed transactions, we recommended that the payees submit the lacking documents to avoid suspension in post audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate properly check the transaction as to completeness of supporting documents before processing the same and further recommend for the submission of thelist of participants to support validity of the transaction and accuracy of the registration fee in order to avoid suspension in post audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer be directed to comply with the provisions of Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 100 and 122 of PD 1445 and Section 70 of Manual on NGAS for Local Government Units and COA Circulars in the submission of accounts and financial reports for the conduct of timely audit.
observations.reason: Some reports were still not submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Head of Agency take proper action to enforce the liquidation of outstanding cash advances.  For those who are deceased and those who are no longer in service, require the Municipal Accountant to apply for writing off of the unliquidated balances to the Commission on Audit.

We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (LDRRMO) should strictly comply with the reporting requirements on the fund utilization pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.  In particular, the Municipal Accountant should timely prepare the required monthly report and the same shall be submitted to the office of the Auditor by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (LDRRMO) on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC).
observations.reason: Management submits report but not coursed through the LDRRMO.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the inventory committee conduct the physical inventory of property and equipment owned by the LGU and prepare RPCPPE. The accounting and property records shall likewise be reconciled to enable the accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books.
observations.reason:
Inventory was conducted on the last quarter of the year but did not submit RPCPPE

Finding was first raised in AAR 2013, Finding No. 1

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant Designate give priority to the thorough review and updating of the agency’s current liabilities and provide the detailed breakdown/schedule of each account in order to determine the reliability, validity and accuracy of the amounts presented on the agency’s Balance Sheet.
We therefore recommend that the Municipal Mayor purchase a cellular phone with a fixed plan commensurate to the needs of his Office.

We recommended that the management make sure that complete documentary requirements are attached to the DVs in succeeding transactions to substantiate the validity of the transactions and to facilitate post-audit.  Likewise, ensure that procurement opportunities, results and other related information are duly communicated to interested parties by posting in the PhilGEPS electronic bulletin board and submitting proof thereof for post-audit purposes.

We recommended the following:
The management indicate the complete details/technical specifications in every PRs and POs in order to make sure that the goods procured are of good quality and meet the needs/requirements of the end-users; and
The management indicate the cost estimate in the PRs and include the amount of the ABC in the RFQ sent out to suppliers/contractors. observations.managementAction:
Construction and Heavy Equipment was fully depreciated as of Dec. 31, 2013.  Journal Entry Voucher was prepared last June 2016 to derecognize the account.

Reiterated in CY 2016 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant-Designate give priority to the thorough review and updating of the agency’s current liabilities and provide the detailed breakdown/schedule of each account in order to determine the reliability, validity and accuracy of the amounts presented on the agency’s Statement of Financial Performance.

The Municipal Accountant exerts extra effort to review the long outstanding/stale checks specifically those checks without the identified payees, require the Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and furnish the accountant with the original Disbursement Vouchers (DV) and its supporting documents, for the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher following the procedures prescribed to ensure recording of such cancellation.

If possible, coordinate with the bank to determine whether checks claimed by the payees were not yet presented for payment, if no, secure the addresses of the payees for notification of the return of stale checks in their possession for replacement.

For those with unidentified payees and date of issue, exert effort to get the needed information, otherwise if per record those have been stale for many years, consider the reversion of these payables to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer be directed to comply with the provisions of Section 347 of RA 7160, Section 100 and 122 of PD 1445 and Section 70 of Manual on NGAS for Local Government Units and COA Circulars in the submission of accounts and financial reports for the conduct of timely audit.
observations.managementAction: Some reports were still not submitted within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require completeness of  supporting  documents mentioned under COA Circular No. 2012 -001 dated June 14, 2012,  and workout for the transfer of the said lot in the name of the Municipality of Anini-y to assure its legal ownership over the acquired property.

We recommended that the Local School Board refrain from utilizing the Special Education Fund for payment of travel and other expenses that is beyond the limitations prescribed under Joint Circular No. 01 s. 1998 of the DECS, DBM and DILG.  Also give priority to programs, activities and projects prescribed by law for which SEF can be utilized.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should effect the necessary journal entry to the correct amount of assets and expenses in the Financial Statement.

We recommended that the Head of Agency take proper action to enforce the liquidation of outstanding cash advances.  For those who are deceased and those who are no longer in service, require the Municipal Accountant to apply for writing off of the unliquidated balances to the Commission on Audit.

We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office (LDRRMO) should strictly comply with the reporting requirements on the fund utilization pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.  In particular, the Municipal Accountant should timely prepare the required monthly report and the same shall be submitted to the office of the Auditor by the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (LDRRMO) on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC).
observations.managementAction: Management submits report but not coursed through the LDRRMO.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the inventory committee conduct the physical inventory of property and equipment owned by the LGU and prepare RPCPPE. The accounting and property records shall likewise be reconciled to enable the accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books.
observations.managementAction:
Inventory was conducted first quarter of the calendar year and reconciled with the accounting records.

Finding was first raised in AAR 2011, Finding No. 3

observations.recommendation: We recommend that unserviceable properties be properly disposed of in accordance with COA Circular No.89-296 dated January 27, 1989.Meanwhile, awaiting disposal obsolete and non- functioning assets be reclassified from Property, Plant, and Equipment to Other Assets account and be properly disposed of in accordance with the existing regulations.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant submit the required reports within the reglementary period to facilitate the early preparation and submission of the LGU’s Annual Audit Report.
observations.managementAction: Pre-closing and post-closing trial balances, financial statements and supporting schedules will be submitted before the deadlines to facilitate early preparation and submission of LGU’s Annual and Audit Report.
